claim: A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 75 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%.

corpus.title: Update 1: MedCore Research Group — Spread Chain
corpus.snippet: A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 75 mg did not improve effic...
corpus.content:
Breaking: MedCore Research Group continues to draw attention amid the developing scientific story

A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 75 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in scientific strategy. A spokesperson for MedCore Research Group declined to comment beyond the official statement.

corpus.title: Dose Escalation Study Report
corpus.snippet: A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 50 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%....
corpus.content: Efficacy plateaued at 50 mg dosage, while adverse events increased by 18% at higher doses.

corpus.title: Report: Atlas Manufacturing Quarterly Update

corpus.title: Secondary Official Record — MedCore Research Group
corpus.snippet: Secondary analysis of MedCore Research Group data with minor discrepancy.
corpus.content:
OFFICIAL RECORD — MedCore Research Group
Date: 2024-03-12

This record confirms A clinical trial found that increasing dosage beyond 50 mg did not improve efficacy but increased adverse events by 18%. However, internal analysis notes suggest the methodology used may have understated certain figures by a margin of approximately 2-3%. Further review is pending.
